title: 11-Foot Barn Door Media Wall
summary: A wall-spanning unit built from ripped plywood strips — two tall towers flanking a center console, closed by an 11-foot barn door — larger than this catalog's solid-1x12 entertainment center.
description:
Every part of this build comes from four sheets of 3/4" plywood ripped into 15-1/4"-wide strips, not solid 1x12 boards — a genuinely different construction from the smaller barn-door entertainment center elsewhere in this catalog, and one that scales this piece to a full 11-foot wall span. Build two identical towers first: pocket-hole each shelf into the sides, using a cut spacer block to keep spacing consistent, with the top shelf's pocket holes facing up and every other shelf's facing down.

**The center console is built the same way as the towers, but its top and center divider need extra attention to hide fasteners** — face the top's pocket holes outward, since the towers will cover them on final assembly, and orient the center divider's visible pocket holes toward whichever side faces away from the room. Attach the whole media wall to studs in the wall once assembled — this piece is meant to be secured, not just set in place.

**Face frames are what make the small assembly imperfections disappear** — measure and cut the 1x6 top/bottom trim and 1x2 stiles to fit your actual assembled dimensions rather than a fixed number, since plywood width and joint variance add up across an 11-foot span. Build the doors from 1x4 (MDF recommended for paint grade) framing a 1/4" plywood panel, install the barn door hardware, then level the rail carefully — an unlevel rail will make the doors drift to one side over time.

tools.note: For ripping the plywood into strips.

materials.name: 3/4" plywood, ripped to 15-1/4" strips, full sheet
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: Measure and cut to fit the assembled unit.
Cut to fit each tower edge and the center divider.

steps.title: Build the towers
steps.body: Drill three pocket holes on each end of every shelf board. Attach to the sides, spacing with a 14" block as a guide — top shelf pocket holes facing up, all others facing down. Add the tower bay supports flush to the back at the top. Build two identical towers.
materials: 3/4" plywood, ripped to 15-1/4" strips, full sheet

steps.title: Build the center console
steps.body: Build the center console the same way, with top pocket holes facing outward to be hidden by the towers, and the center divider's pocket holes facing away from the room if possible.
materials: 3/4" plywood, ripped to 15-1/4" strips, full sheet

steps.title: Join the console and towers
steps.body: Attach the center bay supports to the underside of the center shelves, then attach the center shelves and bay supports to the towers. Secure the whole assembly to studs in the wall.

steps.title: Build the face frame
steps.body: Measure and cut the face frame top/bottom trim and stiles to fit the assembled unit. Attach with brad nails and glue, filling nail holes and sanding smooth.

steps.title: Build the doors
steps.body: Build the door frames from the stiles and rails with pocket holes. Nail the plywood panels to the back of each frame with brad nails and glue.

steps.title: Install the barn door hardware
steps.body: Attach the door hanger hardware to the doors, hold in place to mark the rail location, and install the rail, checking it's level so the doors don't drift.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

images.alt: 11 foot barn door media wall with two towers and a center console
